2603.16377.title: Age Predictors Through the Lens of Generalization, Bias Mitigation, and Interpretability: Reflections on Causal Implications
2603.16377.paper_summary: Chronological age predictors often fail to achieve out-of-distribution (OOD) gen- eralization due to exogenous attributes such as race, gender, or tissue. Learning an invariant representation with respect to those attributes is therefore essential to improve OOD generalization and prevent overly optimistic results. In predic- tive settings, these attributes motivate bias mitigation; in causal analyses, they appear as confounders; and when protected, their suppression leads to fairness. We coherently explore these concepts with theoretical rigor and discuss the scope of an interpretable neural network model based on adversarial representation learning. Using publicly available mouse transcriptomic datasets, we illustrate the behavior of this model relative to conventional machine learning models. We observe that the outcome of this model is consistent with the predictive results of a published study demonstrating the effects of Elamipretide on mouse skeletal and cardiac muscle. We conclude by discussing the limitations of deriving causal interpretation from such purely predictive models.
2603.16377.paper_summary_zh: 時間年齡預測器常常無法實現超出分佈（OOD）的泛化，這是由於種族、性別或組織等外生屬性所致。<lb/>因此，學習一種對這些屬性不變的表示是提高OOD泛化和防止過於樂觀結果的關鍵。<lb/>在預測設定中，這些屬性促使減少偏見；在因果分析中，它們出現為混淆變量；而當受到保護時，它們的抑制則導致公平性。<lb/>我們以理論嚴謹的方式一致地探討這些概念，並討論基於對抗性表示學習的可解釋神經網絡模型的範疇。<lb/>使用公開可用的小鼠轉錄組數據集，我們說明了該模型相對於傳統機器學習模型的行為。<lb/>我們觀察到該模型的結果與一項已發表研究的預測結果一致，該研究展示了Elamipretide對小鼠骨骼和心臟肌肉的影響。<lb/>最後，我們討論了從這些純粹預測模型中推導因果解釋的局限性。
2603.16377.author: Debdas Paul et.al.
2603.16377.authors: Debdas Paul, Elisa Ferrari, Irene Gravili, Alessandro Cellerino
